km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស, uណៃteត គីnហ្គដុម
km.Essex, England: អេសេច, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa於大約1400年出生在英國英格蘭。他和Lady Rosa至少有1個女兒。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa于大约1400年出生在英国英格兰。他和Lady Rosa至少有1个女儿。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Rosa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Antonio, Juan, Angel, Mario, Pedro, Ana, Miguel, Rafael, Ramon.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Adao, Guilherme, Henrique, Paulo, Zulmira.

Italian, Catalan, Spanish, and Portuguese: from rosa ‘rose’ (from Latin rosa), applied as a topographic name for someone who lived where wild roses grew, or as a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or as a nickname for someone with a pink, rosy complexion.

Italian: from the female personal name Rosa, from rosa ‘rose’. Under the influence of the cults of various Christian saints, particularly Rosa, patron saint of Viterbo, this was a popular personal name in medieval Italy. It is also a Marian devotional name (compare Rosario 1). Compare also De Rosa .

variants.name: Rosania
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gustavo, Antonio, Guido, Mario, Nichola, Salvatore.
Italian: apparently from a derivative of   Rosa  .

variants.name: Rosiak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Ignatius, Janina, Marcin.
Polish: patronymic from   Rosa  .

variants.name: Rosario
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Angel, Carlos, Pedro, Manuel, Rafael, Luz, Ramon, Miguel, Julio.  Portuguese Batista, Joao, Vasco.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Carmelo, Dario, Lorenzo, Saturnina, Cecilio, Clemente, Eliseo, Fausto, Federico, Luciano.

Spanish and Portuguese: short form of a surname such as Spanish Del Rosario , or from a short form of the Marian name María del Rosario, given in particular to a girl who was born on the festival of Our Lady of the Rosary, celebrated on the first Sunday in October. The word rosario ‘rosary’ derives from Late Latin rosarium ‘rose garden’, and was transferred to a set of devotions dedicated to the Virgin Mary as the result of the medieval symbolism which constantly compared her to a rose. Compare Rozario .
Italian: from the male personal name Rosario, of the same origin as 1 (above), or a habitational name from a minor place so named.

variants.name: Roosa
meanings: Dutch: cognate of   Rosa   1.

variants.name: Roselli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Luigi, Marco, Carmine, Domenic, Giuseppe, Rinaldo, Angelo, Domenico, Enrico, Ercole, Francesco.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of Rosello, a diminutive of Rosa .

variants.name: La Rosa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Sal, Angelo, Antonio, Domenic, Gasper, Giacomo, Giuseppe, Pasquale, Pietro, Salvator, Santo.  Spanish Mario, Alberto, Jose, Pablo, Alfredo, Carlos, Jesus, Julio, Rosario, Benito, Domingo, Fernando.
Italian (southern; also Larosa): from rosa ‘rose’ (see Rosa ), with the definite article la, a topographic name for someone living by a prominent rose bush.
Spanish: habitational name from any of the places called La Rosa in southern Spain, or a short form of the surname   De la Rosa  .

variants.name: Rosamilia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Alicia, Geraldo, Gerardo, Orlando.
Italian (Avellino): perhaps from a compound of the personal names Rosa and Emilia, or a folk etymologization of something entirely different.

variants.name: Rosas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Jesus, Carlos, Manuel, Ruben, Pedro, Miguel, Roberto, Francisco, Rafael.  Portuguese Agostinho, Gonsalo, Joaquim.  Italian Antonio, Marco, Leonardo, Lorenzo, Carmelo, Filiberto, Bartolo, Cecilio, Elbio, Fausto, Federico, Lucio.
Spanish and Portuguese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Las Rosas, or a topographic name from the plural of rosa ‘rose’ (see Rosa ). Compare De Rosas .
Catalan: Castilianized form of Roses, a habitational name from Roses, a town in L'Alt Empordà, Catalonia.

variants.name: Rosetta
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Nicola.
Italian: from the personal name Rosetta, a pet form of Rosa .

variants.name: Roso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo.
Italian: from a masculinized form of   Rosa  , or a variant of   Rosso  .
Spanish: nickname from roso ‘smooth-faced; bald’. Compare Rozo .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa was born about 1400, in England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Lady Rosa. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa est né environ 1400, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Lady Rosa. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa wurde um 1400 in England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mit Lady Rosa mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa è nato il circa 1400 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Lady Rosa. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Rosaは約1400年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた。彼はLady Rosaとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa(은)는 대략 1400년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났다. 그는 Lady Rosa(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa nasceu aproximadamente 1400, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Lady Rosa. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa родился около 1400 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Lady Rosa. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Rosa nació aproximadamente 1400, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Lady Rosa. Murió en su pueblo.

th.1400–Female: ปี1400–ถึงแก่กรรม
th.1400–Male: ปี1400–ถึงแก่กรรม

th.Essex, England: ส์สเอกซ์, เอิงแลนด์
